summary:
The evidence highlights impaired interpretation abilities in individuals with Feigenbaum-Bergeron-Richardson syndrome, specifically related to social cues and medical information, without mentioning reading comprehension or any form of therapeutic intervention. Since no studies address improvements in reading comprehension or its treatability, the claim that such ability can be enhanced has no basis in the provided findings.  

Thus, based purely on the documented facts, reading comprehension cannot be improved. Final answer: \boxed{B}. 

(Only two sentences as required.)  
The evidence shows significant difficulties in interpreting both social and medical information but makes no reference to reading comprehension or possible improvement via therapy. Thus, based on the provided materials, reading comprehension cannot be improved. \boxed{B}
